relations.note: Charts and tables are explicitly exempted from the signature asymmetric opposite-radius styling, requiring a uniform 12px radius instead.

quotes: callout: 42px & 6px. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right), the 6px radius to the other pair.
A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**; the other two corners keep a slight radius.
Be consistent within an email: same diagonal orientation across all sections

rules.intent: Maintain modern, optimistic brand framing via signature asymmetric accent container styling.
rules.rule_text: The Brand Accent Shape container uses selectively enlarged opposite corner radii. For standard callout boxes in email, this asymmetric radius is configured as 40px 8px 40px 8px (icon_callout.box.radius = "40px 8px 40px 8px" / "42px & 6px" nominal) with consistent diagonal orientation across all sections.

quotes: Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]
Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

rules.intent: Ensure data presentation structures remain clean and legible without asymmetric distortions.
rules.rule_text: As an exception to the asymmetric accent shape, charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (radius.infographic.panel = 12px).
